summary: A small, stepped rectangular pavilion with a raised central tower. The structure sits in a cleared rectangular plot with a low block perimeter; the main mass is a two-tiered rectangular block with chamfered/stepped corners and a decorative flat tiled roof and small stacked tower centered on top.

shape.overall_form: rectangular stepped mass with a centered stacked tower; walkways and low perimeter posts surround it
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (entrance aligned to center), likely symmetrical on both short sides

materials.name: sea_lantern
materials.role: decorative light on small tower (possible beacon-like block)

rebuild_hints: Start from a rectangular base approximately 13x21 blocks. Create a two-tiered solid rectangular volume: lower band of sandstone (about 3–4 blocks tall) with a continuous quartz trim band wrapping around at mid and top heights.
Place quartz corner vertical buttresses that project outward by 1–2 blocks and create a stepped profile on each corner; add a thin brick tiled flat roof inset inside a quartz coping.

Add a centered small tower rising ~3 blocks above the roof: stonebrick (or similar) cap layers with an inner glass windowed shaft and a single light block (sea lantern or glow) on top; surround the building with spaced quartz low posts outlining the plot and a central entrance walkway aligned to the long axis.
uncertainties: Exact block types: sandstone vs smooth_sandstone vs chiseled sandstone not fully distinguishable; quartz likely but could be white concrete/white wool in texture.
Small top light block appears to be a sea_lantern or prismarine block; confidence low.
Exact internal layout and floor count inside the mass are unknown (only exterior visible).
Precise dimensions are estimated from visible block counts and aerial shots; may vary by +/- 2 blocks.
